title: When You Hit Upon A Star

jokes.text: If I had two sets of lips, I'd kiss myself.
jokes.setup: Will admiring his poker hand
jokes.punchline: Absurd declaration about kissing himself with hypothetical extra lips
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith-style absurd bravado. Good character voice and memorable phrasing, though concept isn't groundbreaking.

jokes.text: We'll be melon balling at 0600.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey discussing catering preparations in formal butler manner
jokes.punchline: Using military time and terminology for the mundane task of melon balling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character beat - his formal/military approach to mundane tasks.

jokes.text: She put her bra on one cup at a time just like everybody else.
jokes.setup: Carlton gushing about Michelle Michaels being a goddess
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's dry deflation using underwear logistics
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Philip's practical wisdom delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Subverts celebrity worship cleverly.

jokes.text: I'd pay big money to see that.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's bra comment
jokes.punchline: Carlton's inappropriate eagerness
jokes.explanation: Predictable horny teenage boy response. Gets a laugh from Carlton's earnestness but fairly obvious.

jokes.text: Well, you overlooked one small fact, Carlton. What's that? You weren't invited.
jokes.setup: Carlton dismissing Will's fight night plans
jokes.punchline: Will's devastating exclusion reveal
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-punchline structure with great misdirection. Classic Will putting Carlton in his place moment.

jokes.text: The glasses have spots on them. Oh, my God. Why didn't you come to me sooner?
jokes.setup: Carlton announcing a 'crisis situation'
jokes.punchline: Will's mock-serious response to trivial problem
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that plays on Will's street-smart perspective versus Carlton's privileged problems.

jokes.text: Today, Michelle Michaels, tomorrow, Gloria Estefan. And then, dare I dream, Michael Bolton.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining the importance of the party
jokes.punchline: His ultimate aspiration being Michael Bolton
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character comedy - his earnest worship of the whitest possible music icons. The escalation structure works well.

jokes.text: People act so stupid when celebrities get around. I need a reality check. You, waiter.
jokes.setup: Will criticizing celebrity worship
jokes.punchline: Immediately treating Michelle like a celebrity himself
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic reversal showing Will's hypocrisy. The immediate contradiction creates good comedy.

jokes.text: I think it stinks. I apologize, Miss Michaels. He's from a foreign land.
jokes.setup: Will giving honest opinion about Michelle's music
jokes.punchline: Carlton's panicked damage control with absurd excuse
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Will's honesty vs Carlton's sycophancy. The 'foreign land' excuse is absurdly desperate.

jokes.text: Back to work, Vladimir. Carlton Banks here. I balled your melons.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to impress Michelle
jokes.punchline: His awkward bragging about melon balling
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton awkwardness. The melon balling comment has unintended sexual undertones that make it funnier.

jokes.text: I've been back here. I couldn't hear anything. All I heard was: [mimics muffled sounds]
jokes.setup: Michelle asking if he liked the show
jokes.punchline: Will's honest admission with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Will's honesty combined with physical comedy of mimicking muffled sounds. Good character moment.

jokes.text: You know, Miss Michaels, I thought your album stunk too.
jokes.setup: Carlton previously defending Michelle's music
jokes.punchline: His sudden honesty after she leaves
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Carlton's two-faced behavior. Simple but effective character reveal.

jokes.text: Hey, I checked her coat.
jokes.setup: Will bragging about speaking to Michelle
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's minimal interaction claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey dry humor. Economy of words with maximum deflation of Will's excitement.

jokes.text: I wonder who'll play you in the movie of her life?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's minor interaction with Michelle
jokes.punchline: Carlton's grandiose interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton character comedy - his tendency to romanticize mundane celebrity interactions.

jokes.text: Nothing too fancy. Something in a hatchback will do just fine.
jokes.setup: Will mentioning his upcoming birthday
jokes.punchline: Suggesting a car as a casual gift
jokes.explanation: Classic Will opportunism - treating expensive gifts as casual requests. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Hilary. Oh, that's all right. Don't worry about it. Aunt Viv, you can still surprise me with that red sweater you hiding in the upstairs closet.
jokes.setup: Hilary accidentally revealing the restaurant reservation
jokes.punchline: Will revealing he knows about other surprise elements
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Will's awareness undermines all surprise attempts. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: Are you stalking me?
jokes.setup: Will showing up at Michelle's house
jokes.punchline: Her direct question about his behavior
jokes.explanation: Direct, economical line that cuts through romantic pretense. Gets laughs from bluntness.

jokes.text: Your second song on your third album is so cool. Who was Omar? Is he your boyfriend?
jokes.setup: Ashley meeting Michelle as a fan
jokes.punchline: Her rapid-fire oversharing of fan knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good portrayal of teenage fan behavior. Ashley's enthusiasm creates appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: Pardon my little sister. She doesn't know how to talk to celebrities. So, Mich.
jokes.setup: Will apologizing for Ashley's fan behavior
jokes.punchline: Immediately being overfamiliar himself with 'Mich'
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Will criticizing Ashley then immediately doing worse. Good character hypocrisy.

jokes.text: We're not hip, so we don't know your music. It's nice to meet you anyway, though.
jokes.setup: Philip introducing himself to Michelle
jokes.punchline: His honest admission about not knowing her work
jokes.explanation: Excellent Philip character moment - his straightforward honesty cuts through celebrity worship. Well-crafted and authentic.

jokes.text: Don't think of me as just a manservant, think of me as a pleasure dome.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey introducing himself to Michelle
jokes.punchline: His grandiose self-description
jokes.explanation: Fantastic Geoffrey line - perfectly absurd yet delivered with complete seriousness. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: See, tonight is dime-a-frame bowling night, you know. Top scorer gets those cute little shoes and I just couldn't miss that.
jokes.setup: Michelle inviting Will to her concert
jokes.punchline: Will's fake excuse about bowling priorities
jokes.explanation: Good playing-hard-to-get moment. The specificity of the bowling details makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Listen, she got a private jet. She has yacht. Does she have a brother?
jokes.setup: Will listing Michelle's luxurious possessions
jokes.punchline: Hilary's practical romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary character moment - her gold-digging nature presented as practical inquiry. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: We're going to the Golden Choice Music Awards. They got us sitting between Ike and Tina just in case something flare up.
jokes.setup: Will describing their awards show seating
jokes.punchline: The strategic placement reference to Ike and Tina's volatile relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever reference humor that works on multiple levels. Good cultural knowledge assumption and dark undertone.

jokes.text: No, there's gonna be patchy morning fog with limited visibility.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if the coast is clear
jokes.punchline: Hilary giving an actual weather forecast
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hilary character comedy - her literal interpretation due to her TV weather background. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: All set. I ordered a Sunny the Seal ice-cream cake. His sleek little body is made of chocolatey goodness and he balances a ball of tutti-frutti sherbet on his buttercream nose.
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting cake for Will's party
jokes.punchline: Carlton's overly detailed description of childish cake
jokes.explanation: Pure Carlton - his childlike enthusiasm for elaborate details. The specificity makes it funnier despite verbosity.

jokes.text: You are way too into this.
jokes.setup: Carlton's elaborate cake description
jokes.punchline: Vivian's understated assessment
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response. Economy works well though the line itself isn't particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Jazz, to what do we owe your presence? My guess would be inbreeding.
jokes.setup: Philip asking why Jazz is there
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's brutal genetic insult
jokes.explanation: Savage Geoffrey line with perfect setup-punchline structure. Brutal but clever, excellent character voice and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, no. You don't understand, Mrs. Banks. She don't come in naked.
jokes.setup: Vivian objecting to naked woman in house
jokes.punchline: Jazz's clarification misses the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Jazz's obliviousness to the real issue. Classic character stupidity played well.

jokes.text: Sergai keeps the salon open till 9. I'm gonna see if he can squeeze my head in.
jokes.setup: Hilary leaving the party
jokes.punchline: Her unintentional sexual innuendo about hair appointment
jokes.explanation: Unintentional innuendo from Hilary's obliviousness. Gets laughs from accidental double meaning.

jokes.text: I'm just glad that the Saint Bernard didn't have to suffer.
jokes.setup: Will describing his skiing experience
jokes.punchline: Implication that his bad skiing somehow involved a rescue dog
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of skiing mishap. The unexpectedness of the Saint Bernard reference works well.

jokes.text: Frank and Kathy Lee came over with that little dude Cody. He made bubbles in the Jacuzzi without the jets on.
jokes.setup: Will describing celebrity neighbors in hot tub
jokes.punchline: Implication that Cody defecated in the jacuzzi
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemistic way to reference bodily functions. Good celebrity name-dropping with gross-out humor.

jokes.text: Will, this Maria Shriver... full-figured gal?
jokes.setup: Will mentioning Maria Shriver at the hot tub
jokes.punchline: Philip's inappropriate focus on her physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Philip's priorities. The euphemistic language adds to the humor.

jokes.text: A cleverly orchestrated ruse, bub. And to think, I laid out 50 big ones on your damn cake.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining the surprise party deception
jokes.punchline: His dramatic language and financial complaint
jokes.explanation: Carlton's overly formal language paired with petty financial concerns. Good callback to the elaborate cake.

jokes.text: The one that goes: [Carlton singing Sunny the Seal jingle]
jokes.setup: Carlton describing the cake commercial
jokes.punchline: Him actually performing the jingle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - his childlike enthusiasm expressed through commercial jingle performance.

jokes.text: Damn. And I mean that in the nicest possible way, Hil.
jokes.setup: Will seeing Hilary's bad haircut
jokes.punchline: His attempt to soften his shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat with Will's attempt at damage control. The qualifier makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I look like a troll doll.
jokes.setup: Hilary's bad haircut reveal
jokes.punchline: Her accurate self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual comedy reference. Hilary's rare moment of accurate self-awareness.

jokes.text: Sleep with one eye open.
jokes.setup: Philip suggesting she's overreacting
jokes.punchline: Hilary's ominous threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from vanity concern to murder threat. Classic Hilary over-the-top response.

jokes.text: You mean, which superstar would I choose?
jokes.setup: Will asking which choice Philip would make
jokes.punchline: Philip revealing he'd choose based on the celebrity, not the principle
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Philip's hypocrisy. Reveals he's just as starstruck as anyone.

jokes.text: You mean, which superstar would I choose?
jokes.setup: Will asking if Philip would choose party or slopes with superstar
jokes.punchline: Philip revealing he'd obviously choose the celebrity too
jokes.explanation: Good Uncle Phil hypocrisy moment. Shows he's not above celebrity worship either.

jokes.text: Listen, make sure they don't let you in, okay?
jokes.setup: Carlton asking about the fight party
jokes.punchline: Will's additional insult about Carlton being excluded
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the Carlton exclusion running gag. Will's cruelty is consistently funny.

jokes.text: I got Vegas, you, me... a black dude and a white dude beating each other. Life is good.
jokes.setup: Will expressing contentment with the fight
jokes.punchline: His blunt racial description of the boxing match
jokes.explanation: Good Will observation. The casual racial commentary works for his character.

jokes.text: I got Vegas, you, me... a black dude and a white dude beating each other. Life is good
jokes.setup: Will describes perfect evening
jokes.punchline: Reduces boxing to racial spectacle
jokes.explanation: Edgy racial humor that works because it's Will's perspective on boxing

jokes.text: Rick Benton. We shot a swimsuit video in Tahiti. Guy?
jokes.setup: Rick introducing himself to Will
jokes.punchline: His casual dismissal of Will as 'Guy'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for conflict. Rick's dismissiveness creates immediate tension and comedy.

jokes.text: Yeah, you were the April fool, right?
jokes.setup: Rick mentioning his calendar
jokes.punchline: Will's pun combining April Fools' Day with insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining calendar reference with insult. Sharp, economical, and character-appropriate comeback.

jokes.text: Why don't you and your Motown-revival haircut go back to your seat, shave your chest, shut up and just watch the fight.
jokes.setup: Growing tension between Will and Rick
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate, multi-part insult
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific, creative insults. The 'Motown-revival haircut' is particularly clever.

jokes.text: How did I know he was gonna fall on Sinatra?
jokes.setup: Will describing the fight aftermath
jokes.punchline: Implication that Frank Sinatra was somehow involved in the brawl
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation. The casual mention of Sinatra as collateral damage is perfectly unexpected.

jokes.text: I just gave one to the paramedic.
jokes.setup: Will asking if his ring was the last one she gave out
jokes.punchline: Michelle casually revealing she gives rings to everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and deflation. The casual nature of her ring-giving undermines any romantic significance.

jokes.text: There's 'Untouchable.' There's 'Never Going To Settle.' There's 'Wham Bam Goodbye.'
jokes.setup: Michelle saying he should listen to her songs
jokes.punchline: Will listing song titles that all indicate she's not relationship material
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating revelation structure. Each song title makes the pattern clearer and funnier.

jokes.text: They showed that on TV? From the first shove to when Sinatra put his toupee back on.
jokes.setup: Will surprised his fight was televised
jokes.punchline: Philip's detailed description including Sinatra's toupee
jokes.explanation: Great callback that escalates the absurdity. The toupee detail makes it even more ridiculous.

jokes.text: You trying to say that the only reason I loved her was because she was rich and let me sponge off of her? Then I love you too, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Philip analyzing Will's feelings for Michelle
jokes.punchline: Will's logical conclusion about loving Philip too
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical conclusion joke that perfectly captures Will's opportunistic nature while showing genuine affection for Philip. Excellent structure and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Then I love you too, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Will's previous mock indignation about loving Michelle for her wealth
jokes.punchline: Admitting he'd love Philip for the same reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline. Excellent structure, perfect character logic, maximum economy. Classic Will moment.

jokes.text: We waited an hour and a half. What's a couple more days?
jokes.setup: Will surprised they still did the party after he missed it
jokes.punchline: Carlton's logic about waiting longer
jokes.explanation: Solid Carlton logic but not exceptional. Good character moment showing his persistence.
